title_en: Al-Bukhari and Al-Adab al-Mufrad: Biography and Background
content_en:
Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari (810–870 CE / 194–256 AH) stands as the greatest hadith scholar in Islamic history, universally recognized for his monumental Sahih al-Bukhari. Yet alongside this masterwork, he compiled Al-Adab al-Mufrad — a separate collection devoted entirely to Islamic manners and social conduct — which reveals a different dimension of his scholarly vision.

Al-Bukhari was born in Bukhara, in present-day Uzbekistan, to a family with deep roots in Islamic scholarship. His father Ismail was himself a student of Imam Malik and died while al-Bukhari was still an infant. From childhood al-Bukhari displayed an extraordinary memory, reportedly memorizing over 70,000 hadiths with their complete chains of transmission. He began his travels in search of hadith at age sixteen, journeying throughout Khurasan, Iraq, the Hijaz, Syria, and Egypt over a period of sixteen years.

Al-Adab al-Mufrad was compiled after the completion of al-Jami as-Sahih, representing al-Bukhari's focused attention on a domain he considered insufficiently addressed in general hadith collections: the daily conduct, social ethics, and interpersonal manners that constitute the practical expression of Islamic character. The title translates roughly as 'A Unique Collection on Manners' — the word mufrad (unique, standalone) indicating that this was a self-contained work on a specific theme.

The collection contains approximately 1,322 hadiths organized into 644 chapters. Unlike the Sahih, where al-Bukhari applied his famously stringent authentication criteria — accepting only hadiths with unbroken chains of reliably transmitters who met with each other — the Adab al-Mufrad applies somewhat less restrictive standards. Al-Bukhari included hadiths of varying grades because the subject matter, while important, involves behavioral guidance rather than matters of obligatory ritual where only the highest-grade evidence suffices.

Scholars have debated this methodological difference. Some argue that al-Bukhari was following the established scholarly convention of being more lenient with hadiths on virtuous deeds (fada'il al-a'mal) and ethical conduct than with hadiths on legal rulings (ahkam). Others note that al-Bukhari was demonstrating that ethical refinement rests on an extensive prophetic tradition even when individual transmissions may be weaker than the standards of the Sahih.

The work was transmitted through al-Bukhari's direct students and eventually became one of the most referenced Islamic texts on manners. Its influence extended across all four legal schools and shaped the ethical literature of subsequent centuries.
content_id:
Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari (810–870 M / 194–256 H) berdiri sebagai ulama hadis terbesar dalam sejarah Islam, diakui secara universal atas karya monumentalnya Sahih al-Bukhari. Namun di samping karya agung ini, beliau menyusun Al-Adab al-Mufrad — sebuah koleksi terpisah yang sepenuhnya didedikasikan untuk akhlak dan tata pergaulan Islam — yang mengungkap dimensi lain dari visi keilmuannya.

Al-Bukhari lahir di Bukhara, di wilayah Uzbekistan saat ini, dari sebuah keluarga yang berakar kuat dalam keilmuan Islam. Ayahnya, Ismail, adalah murid Imam Malik sendiri dan wafat ketika al-Bukhari masih bayi. Sejak kecil, al-Bukhari menunjukkan daya ingat yang luar biasa, konon menghafal lebih dari 70.000 hadis beserta seluruh sanad-nya. Beliau memulai perjalanan menuntut ilmu hadis pada usia enam belas tahun, mengembara ke Khurasan, Irak, Hijaz, Suriah, dan Mesir selama enam belas tahun.

Al-Adab al-Mufrad disusun setelah selesainya Al-Jami as-Sahih, mencerminkan perhatian khusus al-Bukhari pada bidang yang beliau anggap kurang dibahas secara memadai dalam koleksi hadis umum: perilaku sehari-hari, etika sosial, dan tata krama dalam pergaulan yang merupakan wujud praktis dari akhlak Islam. Judul kitab ini diterjemahkan secara kasar sebagai "Koleksi Unik tentang Akhlak" — kata mufrad (unik, tersendiri) menunjukkan bahwa ini adalah karya mandiri yang membahas tema tertentu.

Koleksi ini memuat sekitar 1.322 hadis yang tersusun dalam 644 bab. Berbeda dengan kitab Sahih, di mana al-Bukhari menerapkan kriteria autentikasi yang sangat ketat — hanya menerima hadis dengan sanad yang bersambung dari para perawi terpercaya yang saling bertemu — Al-Adab al-Mufrad menerapkan standar yang sedikit lebih longgar. Al-Bukhari memasukkan hadis dengan berbagai tingkatan karena pokok bahasannya, meskipun penting, menyangkut panduan perilaku dan bukan masalah ibadah wajib yang hanya dapat bersandar pada dalil berderajat tertinggi.

Para ulama telah memperdebatkan perbedaan metodologis ini. Sebagian berpendapat bahwa al-Bukhari mengikuti konvensi keilmuan yang telah mapan, yaitu bersikap lebih longgar terhadap hadis tentang amal-amal utama (fada'il al-a'mal) dan akhlak dibandingkan hadis tentang hukum-hukum syariat (ahkam). Sebagian lain mencatat bahwa al-Bukhari sedang menunjukkan bahwa pembinaan akhlak bersandar pada tradisi kenabian yang sangat luas, meskipun riwayat-riwayat individual mungkin lebih lemah dari standar yang diterapkan dalam kitab Sahih.

Kitab ini diriwayatkan melalui murid-murid langsung al-Bukhari dan pada akhirnya menjadi salah satu teks Islam yang paling banyak dirujuk dalam bidang akhlak. Pengaruhnya meluas ke semua empat mazhab fikih dan membentuk literatur etika pada abad-abad berikutnya.
title_id: Al-Bukhari dan Al-Adab al-Mufrad: Biografi dan Latar Belakang
content_ar: محمد ﷺ ibn Ismail البخاري (810–870 CE / 194–256 AH) stands as the greatest الحديث scholar in Islamic history, universally recognized for his monumental صحيح البخاري. Yet alongside this masterwork, جمع Al-Adab al-Mufrad — a separate collection devoted entirely to Islamic الآداب and social السلوك — which reveals a different dimension of his العلمي vision. البخاري was born in Bukhara, in present-day Uzbekistan, to a الأسرة with deep roots in Islamic scholarship. His الأب Ismail was himself a student of الإمام مالك and died بينما البخاري was still an infant. From childhood البخاري displayed an extraordinary memory, reportedly memorizing over 70,000 hadiths with their complete chains of transmission. He began his travels in search of الحديث at age sixteen, journeying throughout Khurasan, Iraq, the Hijaz, Syria, and Egypt over a period of sixteen years. Al-Adab al-Mufrad was compiled بعد the completion of al-Jami as-صحيح, representing البخاري's focused attention on a domain he considered insufficiently addressed in general الحديث collections: the daily السلوك, social ethics, and interpersonal الآداب that constitute the practical expression of Islamic الأخلاق. The title translates roughly as 'A Unique Collection on الآداب' — the word mufrad (unique, standalone) indicating that this was a self-contained work on a specific theme. The collection contains approximately 1,322 hadiths organized into 644 الأبواب. Unlike the صحيح, where البخاري applied his famously stringent authentication criteria — accepting only hadiths with unbroken chains of reliably transmitters who met with بعضهم بعضاً — the Adab al-Mufrad applies somewhat less restrictive standards. البخاري included hadiths of varying grades لأن the subject matter, بينما المهم, involves behavioral الهداية rather than matters of obligatory ritual where only the highest-grade evidence suffices. العلماء have debated this methodological difference. Some argue that البخاري was following the established العلمي convention of being more lenient with hadiths on virtuous deeds (fada'il al-a'mal) and ethical السلوك than with hadiths on legal rulings (ahkam). Others note that البخاري was demonstrating that ethical refinement rests on an extensive prophetic التراث even عندما individual transmissions may be weaker than the standards of the صحيح. The work was transmitted through البخاري's direct students and eventually became one of the most referenced Islamic texts on الآداب. Its influence extended across all four legal schools and shaped the ethical literature of subsequent centuries.
